highlightedText: It's the home of the Danish royal family. You can watch the change of guard at 12:00.

text: The home of the Danish royal family, it's a must-see spot for first-time visitors. The change of guard at 12:00 is a unique experience.
This iconic and picturesque area is known for its colorful buildings and vibrant atmosphere. It's the perfect spot to enjoy the local vibe and take some memorable photos.
As a symbol of Copenhagen and a character from Grimm's Fairy Tales, this statue is a must-visit. It's one of the most photographed statues in the world.
This significant landmark holds both historical and political importance. It provides a deep insight into Danish history.
To understand the rich history and culture of Denmark, a visit to the country’s largest cultural history museum is essential.
Known for its enchanting atmosphere, beautiful gardens, and thrilling amusement park rides, it's a fun and relaxing place to visit. It's a great way to end your day in Copenhagen.
Currency: The official currency in Denmark is the Danish Krone (DKK). Most places do not accept euros, so you'll need to have some local currency on hand.
Transportation: There are in total 6 metros in Copenhagen. Visitors can reach anywhere by metro. It's a convenient and efficient way to get around the city.
The weather in Copenhagen in December can be quite cold, with temperatures below -15 degrees Celsius. Be sure to pack warm clothing.

The official language is Danish, but English is widely spoken in Copenhagen. However, it's always appreciated when visitors try to use a few local phrases. The “hello” in Danish is “hej”, “thank you” is “farvel”, and “goodbye” is “tak”.
Copenhagen Card: You can get a Copenhagen Card that covers 80 attractions and public transports with $30. It's a great way to save money if you plan on visiting many attractions.
Tipping: The tip rate in Copenhagen is over 50%. It's customary to leave a tip in restaurants and for taxi drivers.
Power Sockets: The type of power sockets used in Denmark are Type A and Type K. Make sure to bring an adapter if your devices use different plugs.
Safety: Copenhagen is generally very safe, but like in any major city, it's wise to stay vigilant, especially at night and in crowded areas.
